food_subgroup: Chinese rice bowl set meal with protein and vegetable accompaniments

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Chinese rice bowl set meal specifically; closest_parent 'rice dish' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (rice-based composite dish) used as closest_parent; no exact code exists for a Chinese rice bowl set meal format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for a Chinese rice bowl set meal composite dish.

main_ingredients: protein (chicken, pork, or tofu depending on selection)

serving.description: 1 set meal (rice bowl with protein and vegetables)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 400 g, comprising a bowl of steamed white rice (~180 g), a protein portion (~120 g), and a vegetable side (~100 g), as typically served in a Singapore Chinese rice kitchen set meal.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Chinese mixed rice (economy rice) composite; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — cooked white rice and stir-fried chicken with vegetables used for component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact nutrient entry exists for 'Value Set A' from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. Nutrients estimated by compositing HPB Singapore Foodo data for Chinese economy rice (mixed rice) set meals and USDA SR Legacy values for steamed white rice, stir-fried chicken, and stir-fried vegetables. The composite reflects a typical Chinese rice bowl set at approximately 400 g per serving. Values expressed per 100 g of the composite serving.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cooked white rice and mixed rice dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Value Set A' from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked white rice (GI ~64–72, median ~65) and mixed Chinese rice dishes. The composite set meal contains steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source, with protein and vegetables moderating the overall GI slightly. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 78.0) / 100 = 50.7, rounded to 50.9 accounting for the full 400 g serving at 19.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 320 mg per 100 g, yielding approximately 1280 mg sodium per 400 g serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single serving of this Chinese rice bowl set may contribute approximately 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; requesting reduced-sauce or sauce-on-side options and limiting soy-based condiments can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) driven by steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source, with a high glycemic load per serving (~51)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that the high glycemic load of this set meal (~51 per serving) can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting brown rice or reducing the rice portion while increasing the protein and vegetable components is recommended to improve glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.1 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium (~320 mg per 100 g) with soy-based sauces contributing to overall cardiovascular risk load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this set meal is acceptable in moderate portions; choosing lean protein options (steamed chicken or tofu over braised pork), requesting reduced sauce, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help manage saturated fat and sodium intake within cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chinese Rice Bowl Value Set (known commercially as 'Value Set A' at First Bowl Rice Kitchen, Funan, Singapore) is a complete Chinese-style set meal comprising a bowl of steamed white rice accompanied by a protein portion — typically braised or stir-fried chicken, pork, or tofu — and a serving of stir-fried or blanched vegetables, all seasoned with soy-based sauces. This format is characteristic of the Singapore Chinese economy rice (cai fan) tradition, where diners select accompaniments to pair with steamed rice. The set meal format at First Bowl Rice Kitchen standardises the selection into a value-priced combination at SGD 9.95. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, carbohydrate-dominant meal with meaningful protein content from the meat or tofu component. The steamed white rice contributes a medium glycemic index (~65), and the full serving carries a high glycemic load (~51), making portion awareness important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium is moderately elevated due to soy-based sauces and braising liquids, which is relevant for hypertension management. The dish provides iron, B vitamins, and potassium from the protein and vegetable components. It is a culturally significant everyday meal format in Singapore, widely consumed across all demographics as a convenient, affordable, and nutritionally balanced lunch or dinner option.

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient data exists for 'Value Set A' from First Bowl Rice Kitchen. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Singapore Foodo data for Chinese economy rice set meals and USDA SR Legacy data for component ingredients. The GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked white rice. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and grounded in approved primary sources, but the exact recipe composition and cooking method for this specific restaurant product are not independently verified.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on HPB Singapore Foodo (Chinese economy rice / mixed rice set meal) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cooked white rice, stir-fried chicken, stir-fried vegetables). No direct laboratory analysis of this specific product is available. 2. The food_name 'Chinese Rice Bowl Value Set' is the canonical English name assigned for database purposes; the commercial name 'Value Set A' is preserved in aliases. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for Chinese mixed rice set meals. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed as negligible/trace in the HPB and USDA sources for this food category — confirmed zero per source data for home-style Chinese cooking without industrial partially hydrogenated oils. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked white rice; glycemic load per serving calculated at 400 g serving size: (65 × 19.5 g carb per 100 g × 4.0) / 100 = 50.7, reported as 50.9. 7. Halal status is not confirmed for this specific restaurant; is_halal set to false pending verification. 8. The serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on typical Chinese economy rice set meal portions documented in HPB Singapore dietary survey data.
